vegan-grated-parmesan-cheese.name: Vegan 'grated' Parmesan Cheese
instructions: Combine nutritional yeast, almond meal, and sea salt in a blender. Using short pulses, blend the ingredients just enough to come together.

vegan-banana-oat-pancakes.name: Vegan Banana-oat Pancakes

ingredients.item: Yogurt or dairy-free yogurt (optional) and pure maple syrup

instructions: Grind 1 cup (100 g) oats in a food processor until finely ground but with some texture remaining (think coarse sawdust), about 1 minute.

Peel 1 banana and tear into large pieces. Add to ground oats along with dairy-free milk, baking powder, cinnamon, salt, vanilla, and 3 Tbsp. oil. Process, stopping to scrape down sides as needed, until well combined. Remove bowl from base and stir chocolate chips (if using) and remaining ¼ cup (29 g) oats into batter. Let sit 15 minutes; it should be slightly thickened.
Meanwhile, thinly slice remaining banana and set aside.

Heat 1 tsp. oil in a 10" or 12" nonstick skillet over medium-high, swirling to coat bottom of pan. Working in batches of 2 or 3, depending on the size of your pan and the confidence you have in flipping, scoop in about ¼ cup batter per pancake and flatten slightly with a spatula. Arrange a few reserved banana slices on top, then reduce heat to medium, and cook until set and golden brown underneath, 2–3 minutes. Flip pancakes over and cook on other side until golden brown, 1–2 minutes. Transfer to a large plate. Repeat with remaining batter, adding more oil to pan between batches as needed.

Serve pancakes topped with yogurt (if using) and maple syrup.


Editor’s note: Head this way for more of our best vegan breakfast ideas →

vegan-fudge.name: Vegan Fudge

ingredients.prep: such as Nestle® Toll House Simply Delicious

instructions: Grease an 8-inch square glass baking dish with coconut oil.
Combine chocolate chips, sweetened condensed coconut milk, and 1/4 cup coconut oil in a saucepan. Let sit over low heat, stirring only as needed until chips are melted, 3 to 5 minutes.
Pour mixture into the prepared baking dish. Refrigerate until fudge is firm, 3 to 4 hours, or put in the freezer for a faster setting time.

victors-non-dairy-hawaiian-coconut-mochi.name: Victor's Non-dairy Hawaiian Coconut Mochi Cake

ingredients.item: Tub vegan margarine (such as smart balance®)

instructions: Beat almond milk, sugar, eggs, vegan margarine, and vanilla extract together in a large bowl. Mix in baking powder until no clumps remain.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking pan.
Add sweet rice flour to the batter; mix well. Add 1 cup coconut flakes and pour batter into the prepared pan. Scatter remaining 1/2 cup coconut on top.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown, about 1 hour. Allow to cool and cut into squares.

village-tart-with-olives-florinis-pepper.name: Village Tart With Olives, Florinis Pepper And Basil
village-tart-with-olives-florinis-pepper.original_name: Χωριάτικη τάρτα με ελιά, πιπεριά Φλωρίνης και βασιλικό

ingredients.item: 10 large black olives, unspoiled and chopped
Some flour for the work bench
3 large flurin peppers without seeds, cut in four
10 large black olives, unspoiled and chopped
Some flour for the work bench
3 large flurin peppers without seeds, cut in four

instructions: Preheat the oven first to resists at 200
Shake the flour with salt in a bowl. We're doing a puddle.
Beat the egg, the yolk, the olive oil and 1 tablespoon of water. Add to the flour and the olives.
Ferment until the dough comes off the walls and does not stick in the hands.
On a floured surface, knead for 5 minutes. Cover the dough with membrane and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
We use an oily 22 cm baking pan.
Open the dough to a baking sheet or work with fingers like plastic, covering the base and the walls of the baking pan.
We drill the surface with a fork.
Cover with membrane and freeze for 20 minutes.
Place the chopped peppers on a grill in the preheated oven and bake for 12'-15' until the peel is darkened.
Immediately put them in a bag and leave 10 minutes. Peel them and cut them into thin slices.

Remove the dough from the refrigerator, place a piece of non-stick baking paper on top and add some rice or some legumes as a weight. Bake for 10 minutes. Remove the paper and bake for another 5 minutes.
Lower temperature to 170°C.
Beat in the mixer the cream cheese to fluffy.
Add the feta cheese, eggs and milk and beat to homogenize.
Finally, add the peppers, basil, rub the pepper and try if you need some more salt.
Pour the mixture into the semi-cooked tart and bake for 25 minutes.
